title: Why a Project Initiation Document (PID) Is Essential
summary: A Project Initiation Document provides a clear project foundation, aligning stakeholders, defining objectives, and establishing constraints. It reduces rework, protects against scope creep, and ensures smooth transitions when resources or team members change.
content: A solid start is essential for any project, regardless of size. The PID guides the team early, reduces extra work later, and acts as a safeguard when resources change or new members join. It sets tone, context, expectations, and constraints, helping to prevent projects from going off‑track due to unclear objectives or missing information.
can_answer: Why should I create a PID?
What problems does a PID prevent?
examples: A team that started without a PID missed critical stakeholder approvals, leading to rework.
warnings: Skipping the PID can result in misaligned expectations and project delays.
